meta.title: We can prevent the next epidemic
meta.speaker: George Zaidan
meta.youtubeTitle: We can prevent the next epidemic - George Zaidan

sentences.text: Ebola is one of the deadliest viruses we know of.
sentences.translation: 埃博拉病毒是我们所了解 最致命的病毒之一。

sentences.text: If left untreated, it kills about half of those it infects.
sentences.translation: 如果不进行治疗， 一半受感染的人将死亡。

sentences.text: It can spread through pretty much every fluid your body makes, including blood and sweat.
sentences.translation: 它可以通过你身体产生的 几乎所有液体进行传播，包括血液和汗液。

sentences.text: Even the dead can transmit the disease, often doing so at their own funerals.
sentences.translation: 即使是死人也会传染疾病， 这经常在死者的葬礼上发生。

sentences.text: On December 26th, 2013, a two-year-old boy in southern Guinea got sick.
sentences.translation: 2013 年 12 月 26 日，几内亚 南部一名两岁男童患病。

sentences.text: Just two days later, he died.
sentences.translation: 仅仅过了两天，他就离开了人世。

sentences.text: It took local doctors working with the international community four months to discover that Ebola was to blame, largely because it had never before been detected outside of Central Africa.
sentences.translation: 当地医生和国际团队经过 4 个月的合作，才发现这是埃博拉病毒所导致的，很大程度上是因为其从来没有在 中非之外的地方被发现过。

sentences.text: In those four months, Ebola gained a head start that would prove devastating.
sentences.translation: 在之后的 4 个月里，埃博拉病毒开始 迅速蔓延，逐渐转为毁灭性的。

sentences.text: The outbreak lasted two years and mushroomed into the largest Ebola epidemic in recorded history.
sentences.translation: 大爆发持续了两年，并迅速成为有史以来规模最大的埃博拉疫情。

sentences.text: More than 28,000 people contracted the disease and over 11,000 died.
sentences.translation: 超过 28,000 人感染了病毒， 死者超过了 11,000 人。

sentences.text: In 2013, Guinea had no formal emergency response system, few trained contact tracers, and no rapid tests, border screenings, or licensed vaccine for Ebola.
sentences.translation: 在 2013 年，几内亚并没有正式的 应急体系来应对埃博拉病毒，很少有经训练的疾控人员， 也没有快速测试，边境筛查，或获得许可的疫苗。

sentences.text: After that epidemic, Guinea, with the support of the US and other international partners, completely overhauled their epidemic response system.
sentences.translation: 在疫情过后，几内亚在美国和国际伙伴的帮助下，对他们的流行病应急系统 进行了彻底的变革。

sentences.text: And in January 2021, that system faced its first real test.
sentences.translation: 在 2021 年 1 月，这个系统迎来了它的第一次考验。

sentences.text: It started when a nurse in southern Guinea developed a headache, vomiting, and fever.
sentences.translation: 在几内亚南部，一个护士出现了 头痛、呕吐和发烧的症状。

sentences.text: A few days later, she died.
sentences.translation: 几天之后，她因病去世，

sentences.text: As dictated by traditional burial practices, her family prepared her body for the funeral.
sentences.translation: 按照传统的葬礼的习俗，她的家人准备她的遗体进行安葬。

sentences.text: Within a week, the nurse’s husband and other family members started experiencing symptoms.
sentences.translation: 在一周之内，她的丈夫和其他 家庭成员也出现了类似的症状。卫生官员比 2013 年更迅速地 怀疑埃博拉病毒，并进行了检测。

sentences.text: Health officials suspected Ebola much quicker than in 2013 and ordered tests.
sentences.translation: 检测结果为阳性，

sentences.text: They came back positive, and Guinea activated its epidemic alert system the next day.
sentences.translation: 第二天，几内亚启动了流行病警报系统，之后，各方反应非常迅速。

sentences.text: Then, lots of things happened very quickly.
sentences.translation: 几内亚国家卫生安全局 启动了 38 个区级应急行动中心，

sentences.text: Guinea’s National Agency for Health Security activated 38 district-level emergency operations centers, as well as a national one.
sentences.translation: 以及一个国家应急行动中心。流行病学家和疾控团队 开始了艰苦的工作，找出谁在什么地方接触病毒；

sentences.text: Teams of epidemiologists and contact tracers began the painstaking job of figuring out exactly who was exposed and when, generating a list of 23 initial contacts that quickly grew to over 1,100.
sentences.translation: 他们列出了一份含有 23 人 一级接触者的名单；这很快增至 1,100 人。最先爆发疫情的城市 迅速设立了快速检测体系。在几内亚、利比里亚和 科特迪瓦的边境，

sentences.text: Advanced rapid testing capacity spun up in the city where the outbreak started.
sentences.translation: 公共卫生工作者筛查了 超过 2 百万位旅行者。

sentences.text: At Guinea’s borders with Liberia and Cote d’Ivoire, public health workers screened more than 2 million travelers.
sentences.translation: 一次大规模的疫苗接种项目展开；最终，超过 900 名社区志愿者 给人们发出疫情警报，

sentences.text: A large-scale vaccination campaign was started.
sentences.translation: 并建议采用其他被大众接受的 埋葬方式来处置死者，

sentences.text: And, finally, more than 900 community mobilizers alerted people of the outbreak and suggested alternative burial practices that were acceptable to the community and reduced the risk of spreading Ebola.
sentences.translation: 这种做法减少了 埃博拉病毒的传播风险。得益于这些措施，2021 年的大爆发在 仅仅 4 个月后就结束了。仅有 23 人感染了埃博拉病毒， 其中仅 12 人死亡。

sentences.text: Thanks to all these measures, the 2021 outbreak ended just four months after it began.
sentences.translation: 这还不到之前大爆发的百分之一。用来控制 2021 年大爆发的 花销是一亿美金，

sentences.text: Only 23 people contracted Ebola; only 12 died.
sentences.translation: 这听起来很多，但与 前一次埃博拉疫情中

sentences.text: That's less than 1% of the deaths in the prior outbreak.
sentences.translation: 全球 530 亿美元的 经济损失相形见绌。

sentences.text: The 2021 outbreak cost $100 million to control— which sounds like a lot but pales in comparison to the global economic cost of the previous outbreak: $53 billion.
sentences.translation: 530 亿美元！那么，是不是所有国家 都应该效仿几内亚的方法呢？并不是。迅速应对流行病大爆发总是很重要的，

sentences.text: So should every country just copy Guinea’s approach?
sentences.translation: 早期预警系统也是不可或缺的。

sentences.text: Not exactly.
sentences.translation: 但除此之外，一个成功的反应 因不同疾病或者不同国家而各不相同。

sentences.text: It is always important to respond to an outbreak quickly, so an early warning system is essential.
sentences.translation: 例如，巴西迅速消灭了 通过蚊子传播的黄热病爆发，这主要是通过大规模的 疫苗接种实现的。

sentences.text: But beyond that, a successful response can look very different for different diseases in different countries.
sentences.translation: 这种方法对巴西很有效，因为巴西是世界上 黄热病疫苗的生产大国，

sentences.text: For example, Brazil quenched an outbreak of yellow fever, which is spread by mosquitoes, primarily by mounting a massive vaccination campaign.
sentences.translation: 而且巴西民众已经 习惯于定期的常规疫苗接种。但是对付很多疾病 并不需要大规模的疫苗接种。2021 年 8 月，一名卡车司机在 布基纳法索检测出霍乱阳性，

sentences.text: That strategy worked well for Brazil because it’s one of the major global producers of the yellow fever vaccine, and its population was accustomed to regular, routine vaccinations.
sentences.translation: 卫生工作人员在当天就对政府发出了警告，并且开始追踪接触者。霍乱是由细菌引起的，

sentences.text: But for many diseases, you don't even need a mass vaccination program.
sentences.translation: 因此，布基纳法索为那些接触 或可能接触的人提供了抗生素。

sentences.text: In August of 2021, a truck driver tested positive for cholera in Burkina Faso.
sentences.translation: 这种极其迅速的反应在几个星期内 阻止了疫情的爆发。

sentences.text: Health care workers alerted the government that same day and contact tracing began immediately.
sentences.translation: 霍乱经常出现在在西非，在 2021 年，有超过 100,000 例 霍乱，超过3,700人死亡。

sentences.text: Cholera is caused by a bacterium, so Burkina Faso gave antibiotics to those exposed or potentially exposed.
sentences.translation: 但是因为布基纳法索准备充分， 其在当年没有死亡病例。一个也没有！

sentences.text: This extremely fast response stopped the outbreak just a few weeks after it started.
sentences.translation: 在泰国清迈，卫生官员试行了一个社区自建 并由社区驱动的疫情警报系统，

sentences.text: Cholera often rears its head in West Africa— in 2021, there were over 100,000 cases and more than 3,700 deaths.
sentences.translation: 来监控动物的健康。这很重要，因为一些动物疫情 可能会转变为人类疫情爆发。

sentences.text: Because Burkina Faso was so well prepared, they had zero deaths that year.
sentences.translation: 村民们使用应用程序 给卫生局上报动物疫情的信息，

sentences.text: Zero.
sentences.translation: 在 16 个月内，发现了 36 起动物疫情。

sentences.text: In Chiang Mai, Thailand, health officials piloted a community-owned, community-driven outbreak alert system to monitor animal health— that’s important because some animal outbreaks have the potential to spill over and become human outbreaks.
sentences.translation: 为了保证任何疫情应对系统的有效性，它需要被大家信任、重视、 并最终在社区中使用。这意味着用当地的语言接触那里的居民，并了解他们的文化、 信仰和他们的生活习惯。最重要的大概是， 我们不能消极怠工，

sentences.text: Villagers used an app to alert health authorities about outbreaks in animals.
sentences.translation: 在疫情到来时才进入状态。

sentences.text: Over the course of 16 months, 36 animal outbreaks were identified.
sentences.translation: 拯救生命的最佳方式之一 是对于卫生基础设施的持久投资，

sentences.text: For any outbreak response system to be effective, it needs to be trusted, valued, and ultimately used by communities.
sentences.translation: 一年 365 天，对每个人的投入，特别是我们当中易感染的人。

sentences.text: That means reaching people where they are, in the language they speak, and aware of the culture, beliefs, and practices with which they live.
sentences.translation: 它需要被大家信任、重视、 并最终在社区中使用。这意味着用当地的语言接触那里的居民，并了解他们的文化、 信仰和他们的生活习惯。最重要的大概是， 我们不能消极怠工，

sentences.text: Perhaps most importantly, we can't expect to do nothing for years and then just swing into action when an outbreak occurs.
sentences.translation: 并了解他们的文化、 信仰和他们的生活习惯。最重要的大概是， 我们不能消极怠工，在疫情到来时才进入状态。拯救生命的最佳方式之一 是对于卫生基础设施的持久投资，

sentences.text: One of the best ways to save lives is to invest in lasting health infrastructure, 365 days a year, for everyone, especially the most vulnerable among us.
sentences.translation: 在疫情到来时才进入状态。拯救生命的最佳方式之一 是对于卫生基础设施的持久投资，一年 365 天，对每个人的投入，特别是我们当中易感染的人。

cues.text: Ebola is one of the deadliest viruses we know of.
If left untreated, it kills about half of those it infects.
It can spread through pretty much every fluid your body makes,
including blood and sweat.
Even the dead can transmit the disease, often doing so at their own funerals.
On December 26th, 2013, a two-year-old boy in southern Guinea got sick.
Just two days later, he died.
It took local doctors working with the international community
four months to discover that Ebola was to blame,
largely because it had never before been detected outside of Central Africa.
In those four months, Ebola gained a head start that would prove devastating.
The outbreak lasted two years
and mushroomed into the largest Ebola epidemic in recorded history.
More than 28,000 people contracted the disease and over 11,000 died.
In 2013, Guinea had no formal emergency response system,
few trained contact tracers, and no rapid tests,
border screenings, or licensed vaccine for Ebola.
After that epidemic, Guinea,
with the support of the US and other international partners,
completely overhauled their epidemic response system.
And in January 2021, that system faced its first real test.
It started when a nurse in southern Guinea developed a headache, vomiting, and fever.
A few days later, she died.
As dictated by traditional burial practices,
her family prepared her body for the funeral.
Within a week, the nurse’s husband and other family members
started experiencing symptoms.
Health officials suspected Ebola much quicker than in 2013 and ordered tests.
They came back positive,
and Guinea activated its epidemic alert system the next day.
Then, lots of things happened very quickly.
Guinea’s National Agency for Health Security
activated 38 district-level emergency operations centers,
as well as a national one.
Teams of epidemiologists and contact tracers began the painstaking job
of figuring out exactly who was exposed and when,
generating a list of 23 initial contacts that quickly grew to over 1,100.
Advanced rapid testing capacity spun up in the city where the outbreak started.
At Guinea’s borders with Liberia and Cote d’Ivoire,
public health workers screened more than 2 million travelers.
A large-scale vaccination campaign was started.
And, finally, more than 900 community mobilizers
alerted people of the outbreak
and suggested alternative burial practices that were acceptable to the community
and reduced the risk of spreading Ebola.
Thanks to all these measures,
the 2021 outbreak ended just four months after it began.
Only 23 people contracted Ebola; only 12 died.
That's less than 1% of the deaths in the prior outbreak.
The 2021 outbreak cost $100 million to control—
which sounds like a lot but pales in comparison
to the global economic cost of the previous outbreak:
$53 billion.
So should every country just copy Guinea’s approach?
Not exactly.
It is always important to respond to an outbreak quickly,
so an early warning system is essential.
But beyond that, a successful response can look very different
for different diseases in different countries.
For example, Brazil quenched an outbreak of yellow fever,
which is spread by mosquitoes,
primarily by mounting a massive vaccination campaign.
That strategy worked well for Brazil
because it’s one of the major global producers of the yellow fever vaccine,
and its population was accustomed to regular, routine vaccinations.
But for many diseases, you don't even need a mass vaccination program.
In August of 2021, a truck driver tested positive for cholera in Burkina Faso.
Health care workers alerted the government that same day
and contact tracing began immediately.
Cholera is caused by a bacterium,
so Burkina Faso gave antibiotics to those exposed or potentially exposed.
This extremely fast response stopped the outbreak
just a few weeks after it started.
Cholera often rears its head in West Africa—
in 2021, there were over 100,000 cases and more than 3,700 deaths.
Because Burkina Faso was so well prepared, they had zero deaths that year.
Zero.
In Chiang Mai, Thailand, health officials piloted
a community-owned, community-driven outbreak alert system
to monitor animal health—
that’s important because some animal outbreaks have the potential
to spill over and become human outbreaks.
Villagers used an app to alert health authorities about outbreaks in animals.
Over the course of 16 months, 36 animal outbreaks were identified.
For any outbreak response system to be effective,
it needs to be trusted, valued, and ultimately used by communities.
That means reaching people where they are, in the language they speak,
and aware of the culture, beliefs, and practices with which they live.
Perhaps most importantly, we can't expect to do nothing for years
and then just swing into action when an outbreak occurs.
One of the best ways to save lives is to invest in lasting health infrastructure,
365 days a year, for everyone,
especially the most vulnerable among us.

cuesZh.text: 埃博拉病毒是我们所了解 最致命的病毒之一。
如果不进行治疗， 一半受感染的人将死亡。
它可以通过你身体产生的 几乎所有液体进行传播，
包括血液和汗液。
即使是死人也会传染疾病， 这经常在死者的葬礼上发生。
2013 年 12 月 26 日，几内亚 南部一名两岁男童患病。
仅仅过了两天，他就离开了人世。
当地医生和国际团队经过 4 个月的合作，
才发现这是埃博拉病毒所导致的，
很大程度上是因为其从来没有在 中非之外的地方被发现过。
在之后的 4 个月里，埃博拉病毒开始 迅速蔓延，逐渐转为毁灭性的。
大爆发持续了两年，
并迅速成为有史以来规模最大的埃博拉疫情。
超过 28,000 人感染了病毒， 死者超过了 11,000 人。
在 2013 年，几内亚并没有正式的 应急体系来应对埃博拉病毒，
很少有经训练的疾控人员， 也没有快速测试，
边境筛查，或获得许可的疫苗。
在疫情过后，
几内亚在美国和国际伙伴的帮助下，
对他们的流行病应急系统 进行了彻底的变革。
在 2021 年 1 月，这个系统迎来了它的第一次考验。
在几内亚南部，一个护士出现了 头痛、呕吐和发烧的症状。
几天之后，她因病去世，
按照传统的葬礼的习俗，
她的家人准备她的遗体进行安葬。
在一周之内，她的丈夫和其他 家庭成员也出现了类似的症状。
卫生官员比 2013 年更迅速地 怀疑埃博拉病毒，并进行了检测。
检测结果为阳性，
第二天，几内亚启动了流行病警报系统，
之后，各方反应非常迅速。
几内亚国家卫生安全局 启动了 38 个区级应急行动中心，
以及一个国家应急行动中心。
流行病学家和疾控团队 开始了艰苦的工作，
找出谁在什么地方接触病毒；
他们列出了一份含有 23 人 一级接触者的名单；这很快增至 1,100 人。
最先爆发疫情的城市 迅速设立了快速检测体系。
在几内亚、利比里亚和 科特迪瓦的边境，
公共卫生工作者筛查了 超过 2 百万位旅行者。
一次大规模的疫苗接种项目展开；
最终，超过 900 名社区志愿者 给人们发出疫情警报，
并建议采用其他被大众接受的 埋葬方式来处置死者，
这种做法减少了 埃博拉病毒的传播风险。
得益于这些措施，
2021 年的大爆发在 仅仅 4 个月后就结束了。
仅有 23 人感染了埃博拉病毒， 其中仅 12 人死亡。
这还不到之前大爆发的百分之一。
用来控制 2021 年大爆发的 花销是一亿美金，
这听起来很多，但与 前一次埃博拉疫情中
全球 530 亿美元的 经济损失相形见绌。
530 亿美元！
那么，是不是所有国家 都应该效仿几内亚的方法呢？
并不是。
迅速应对流行病大爆发总是很重要的，
早期预警系统也是不可或缺的。
但除此之外，一个成功的反应 因不同疾病或者不同国家而各不相同。
例如，巴西迅速消灭了 通过蚊子传播的黄热病爆发，
这主要是通过大规模的 疫苗接种实现的。
这种方法对巴西很有效，
因为巴西是世界上 黄热病疫苗的生产大国，
而且巴西民众已经 习惯于定期的常规疫苗接种。
但是对付很多疾病 并不需要大规模的疫苗接种。
2021 年 8 月，一名卡车司机在 布基纳法索检测出霍乱阳性，
卫生工作人员在当天就对政府发出了警告，
并且开始追踪接触者。
霍乱是由细菌引起的，
因此，布基纳法索为那些接触 或可能接触的人提供了抗生素。
这种极其迅速的反应在几个星期内 阻止了疫情的爆发。
霍乱经常出现在在西非，
在 2021 年，有超过 100,000 例 霍乱，超过3,700人死亡。
但是因为布基纳法索准备充分， 其在当年没有死亡病例。
一个也没有！
在泰国清迈，
卫生官员试行了一个社区自建 并由社区驱动的疫情警报系统，
来监控动物的健康。
这很重要，因为一些动物疫情 可能会转变为人类疫情爆发。
村民们使用应用程序 给卫生局上报动物疫情的信息，
在 16 个月内，发现了 36 起动物疫情。
为了保证任何疫情应对系统的有效性，
它需要被大家信任、重视、 并最终在社区中使用。
这意味着用当地的语言接触那里的居民，
并了解他们的文化、 信仰和他们的生活习惯。
最重要的大概是， 我们不能消极怠工，
在疫情到来时才进入状态。
拯救生命的最佳方式之一 是对于卫生基础设施的持久投资，
一年 365 天，对每个人的投入，
特别是我们当中易感染的人。
